title: Weird and wonderful sea pen found on Mystery Ridge
description: An ongoing census of sea life in the South Atlantic has identified over 1000 new creatures, including a new species of sea pen
author: Michael Dalton

title: Space storms could switch train signals and cause serious accidents
description: Critical safety equipment in many train systems is vulnerable to disruption by space weather, which could lead to fatal accidents
author: Michael Le Page

title: Earliest use of anaesthetics uncovered in Chinese doctor’s tomb
description: Residues on medical equipment reveal that physicians in China over 600 years ago used aconitine, a highly toxic plant chemical, to alleviate pain during surgical procedures
author: James Woodford

title: Attack on Iran’s oil released as much pollution as a volcano
description: Airstrikes on Tehran earlier this year emitted a plume containing almost 30,000 tonnes of sulphur dioxide that reached Asian countries
author: Alec Luhn

title: Mars astronauts may do laundry by blasting clothes with a plasma beam
description: There is currently no good way for astronauts in space to do laundry, but researchers may have finally come up with one: a bright purple jet of microbe-killing plasma
author: Leah Crane

title: Mercury may have gained all of its unexpected water in a single day
description: Despite being the closest planet to the sun, Mercury has thick deposits of ice at its poles, and now we may understand the events that formed them over just one Mercurian day
author: Leah Crane
